methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Reality Defender is a Series B–stage AI & Software company founded by Ben Colman and Gaurav Bharaj, having raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom), with a public comparable (Palantir) informing sector context.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $50M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $95M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable Series B companies yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). The wide dispersion—driven primarily by SSI's outlier positioning—reflects heterogeneity in capability maturity and investor appetite within frontier AI.

Triangulation supports a range of $81M to $5.8B, with a median of $2.5B. The stage-progression method anchors a conservative floor; peer-median analysis dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include Reality Defender's product maturity and differentiation relative to the peer cohort, particularly given the extreme variance in comparable valuations. The analysis is most reliable if Reality Defender's stage readiness and market position align with mid-to-lower-quartile peers rather than category leaders like Safe Superintelligence.
